label: human
text: I spent three weeks debugging a memory leak in our Go service. Turned out it was a goroutine that never got canceled because someone forgot a ctx.Done() check in a for-select loop. The fix was two lines. I wanted to scream.

label: human
text: Look, I don't care what the benchmarks say. SQLite handles 90% of what people use Postgres for and it's a single file. No daemon, no connection pooling drama, no pg_hba.conf nightmares at 2am. Fight me.

label: human
text: My wife asked me why I was mass-renaming files at midnight. I said 'regex practice.' She didn't buy it. Honestly I was just procrastinating on the actual feature I'm supposed to ship tomorrow.

label: human
text: Hey Sarah - quick heads up, the deploy is gonna be late. Jenkins is being Jenkins again and the docker build keeps timing out. I'll ping you when it's up. Probably after lunch.

label: human
text: The dirty secret of microservices is that most teams would be better off with a well-structured monolith. I've seen companies with 12 engineers running 40 services. That's not architecture, that's a staffing problem disguised as technical sophistication.

label: human
text: Writing good error messages is a design problem, not an engineering problem. 'Error: ECONNREFUSED' tells you what happened to the computer. 'Can't reach the database - is it running?' tells you what happened to you.

label: human
text: Tried Rust for a weekend project. Borrow checker kicked my ass for 6 hours straight. Then something clicked and I rewrote the whole thing in 45 minutes. Still not sure if I like it or if it's Stockholm syndrome.

label: human
text: Our on-call rotation is broken. Last month I got paged 14 times in one week, 9 of which were false alarms from a threshold someone set in 2019 and nobody remembers why. We're fixing this by actually deleting alerts that haven't fired a real incident in 6 months.

label: human
text: Just got out of the planning meeting. We're cutting scope on the dashboard rewrite — no more real-time charts in v1. Tom pushed back but honestly we can't ship anything if we keep gold-plating it. I'll update the Jira board after lunch.

label: human
text: The best code review I ever got was three words: 'delete this file.' The reviewer was right. I'd built an entire abstraction layer for a problem we didn't have yet, and probably never would. Sometimes the hardest engineering skill is not engineering.

label: human
text: I keep a file called mistakes.md in every project. Not retrospectives, not post-mortems — just a running list of dumb things I did. Like when I deployed to prod instead of staging because I had two terminal tabs open. Humbling but useful.

label: human
text: PSA: if you're using Python 3.12+ there's literally no reason to write your own dataclass boilerplate anymore. TypedDict + Pydantic v2 handles everything. I wasted two days writing custom serializers last month before a coworker pointed this out.

label: human
text: We switched from Kubernetes to just... running stuff on EC2 with systemd. Costs dropped 60%. Deploys went from 8 minutes to 40 seconds. Nobody on the team actually understood the Helm charts anyway. Boring technology wins again.

label: human
text: Technical debt isn't a metaphor most engineers take seriously enough. Actual debt has interest rates. You can calculate it. But when someone says 'we'll refactor later,' nobody puts a dollar figure on what 'later' costs per sprint. Maybe we should.

label: human
text: Genuine question — does anyone actually read the AWS documentation or do we all just google the error message and click the first Stack Overflow link? Asking for a friend (the friend is me).

label: human
text: I interviewed at a company that wanted 5 years of Kubernetes experience in 2019. Kubernetes was released in 2014. The recruiter didn't see the problem. That's when I stopped taking job requirements literally.

label: human
text: Heads up — I'm taking Friday off. The PR for the auth refactor is ready for review, tests pass locally. If the CI flakes on the integration tests just re-run, it's the Stripe sandbox being flaky again. Have a good weekend.

label: human
text: The problem with most monitoring dashboards isn't too few metrics, it's too many. When everything is a graph, nothing is a signal. We went from 200 panels to 12 and our incident response time actually improved because people could find what mattered.

label: human
text: Pair programming works when both people want to be there. Mandatory pairing is just surveillance with extra steps. I've had great pairing sessions and I've had sessions where we both stared at the screen pretending to think. Context matters.

label: human
text: Hot take: most yaml files are just poorly typed programming languages with worse error messages. I say this as someone who has written about 8000 lines of yaml this quarter for various CI pipelines. I need help.

label: human
text: Burned 4 hours today chasing a CSS bug that turned out to be a missing semicolon. The layout looked fine in Chrome, broken in Firefox, and completely bonkers in Safari. Some days I question my career choices.

label: human
text: Nobody talks about how much software engineering is reading other people's code. The bootcamps teach you to write code. The job is 80% reading code someone else wrote three years ago with no documentation and variable names like 'tmp2'.

label: human
text: Updated my resume for the first time in 4 years. Apparently I need to list 'AI/ML experience' now even though the closest I've come to ML is running a logistic regression in a Jupyter notebook once. The job market is wild.

label: human
text: We built a fancy ML recommendation engine that took 3 months. Then we A/B tested it against 'show the most popular items sorted by recency.' The dumb version won. By a lot. We shipped the sort.

label: human
text: Can you take a look at the migration script before I run it? It touches the users table and I'd rather have a second pair of eyes. Last time someone ran a migration without review we lost the email column for about 20 minutes. No rush, sometime today is fine.
